author: gb9t
content:
Default-policy refinement locked-in 2026-05-09 (user direction):

**Each absorbed row-1 grsec/PaX feature defaults ON and is configurable.**

Implementation: every feature in the security/00-overview.md absorption table now has an explicit 'Default + configurability' column. The configurability mechanism is sysctl, Kconfig, or per-process prctl as appropriate per feature.

Three operational categories:

MANDATORY (cannot disable — type-system enforced or architectural):
KERNEXEC, UDEREF, USERCOPY, REFCOUNT, AUTOSLAB, PRIVATE_KSTACKS, SIZE_OVERFLOW, CONSTIFY, PAGEEXEC, CLOSE_KERNEL/CLOSE_USERLAND.

DEFAULT-ON, CONFIGURABLE OFF (sysctl/Kconfig knob; userspace-invisible or matches upstream defaults):
RAP/CFI, RANDSTRUCT (internal structs only), RANDKSTACK, MEMORY_SANITIZE (zero on free), LATENT_ENTROPY, NOEXEC (default mode), DIRECT_CALL/Spectre-v2, RANDMMAP, ASLR, DELAY_FREE_ONE_PAGE.

Several of these flip Rookery's defaults from upstream's:
- RANDKSTACK: upstream CONFIG_RANDOMIZE_KSTACK_OFFSET=n; Rookery =y + sysctl=1
- MEMORY_SANITIZE (init_on_free): upstream default off; Rookery default on
- LATENT_ENTROPY: upstream gcc-plugin-default-off; Rookery default on
- DELAY_FREE_ONE_PAGE: not in upstream; Rookery default on

DEFAULT-OFF AT SYSTEM LEVEL, OPT-IN PER-PROCESS (the JIT carve-out):
NOEXEC strict, MPROTECT-W→X-block.

Rationale for the carve-out: default-on system-wide breaks Chrome/Firefox (V8 + SpiderMonkey JIT), OpenJDK HotSpot, .NET CLR, Erlang BEAM, LuaJIT, PyPy. Per-process opt-in via existing prctl(PR_SET_MDWE) is preserved; systemd's MemoryDenyWriteExecute=yes propagates this. New question Q3 in security/00-overview.md asks whether to invert the default with a per-process exemption mechanism — recommended path but requires defining a new ELF note + prctl + distro-side patching of JIT runtimes.

Updates:
- 00-overview.md Foundational Decisions Hardening source row updated to state default-policy refinement
- security/00-overview.md absorption table now has 4 columns including the Default policy
- security/00-overview.md adds Q3 (MPROTECT default-inversion) to the open list
- Knowledge pages refreshed

Both new defaults policy + JIT carve-out compatible with REQ-1 drop-in compat: distros' JIT-using userspace continues to work; the strong defaults apply to everything else; per-process opt-in is the existing MDWE mechanism so no new userspace ABI is required for the baseline.
